title: Door Lock Failure
shortDescription: The washing machine door lock mechanism has failed to engage or the control board cannot confirm that the door is securely locked.
fullDescription: Error code E01 on Beko washing machines indicates a problem with the door locking mechanism. The machine will not start a cycle because the electronic door lock (interlock) has not engaged within the expected time, or the control board is not receiving the confirmation signal. This is a safety feature to prevent the door from opening during operation. The fault may lie in the door lock actuator, the wiring harness, the door latch mechanism, or the main control board.

causes.cause: Door lock actuator (interlock) has failed electrically
Door latch or strike is misaligned or broken
Wiring harness between door lock and control board is damaged
Main control board door lock circuit has failed
Door hinge is bent causing improper door closure

symptoms: Machine does not start when the start button is pressed
Door does not click or lock when closed
E01 error code displayed on control panel
Machine begins to fill with water but stops immediately
Door lock makes a buzzing sound but does not engage

fixSteps.title: Check the door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door latch and strike plate. Ensure the door closes fully and the latch clicks into place. Look for any visible damage, warping, or obstruction preventing full closure.

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washing machine from the mains for 2 minutes. Plug it back in and attempt to start a cycle. This can clear a temporary electronic glitch.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the front panel or retaining band to access the door lock. Disconnect the wiring connector and test continuity across the lock terminals with a multimeter. The lock should show continuity when activated (apply 220V briefly with caution or test with component tester).

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the wiring from the door lock connector back to the main PCB. Look for chafed, burned, or disconnected wires. Pay special attention to the area near the door hinge where wires flex repeatedly.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: If the interlock tested faulty, order a compatible Beko door lock. Remove the door seal retaining band, peel back the rubber seal, unscrew the lock from its mounting, disconnect the wiring, and fit the new lock in reverse order.

whenToCallPro: If the control board is suspected of being faulty or if you are not comfortable working with mains-voltage door lock components.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E01 error door lock fix

faq.q: Can I override the E01 error and still use the machine?
faq.a: No. The door lock is a critical safety mechanism. Running the machine with a faulty lock could result in the door opening during a spin cycle, causing water damage and injury.

faq.q: Is E01 always a door lock problem?
faq.a: Usually yes, but in rare cases it can be caused by a faulty control board that is not sending the lock signal. Start by testing the lock itself before suspecting the PCB.

faq.q: How long does a Beko door lock last?
faq.a: Typically 5-8 years with normal use. Frequent slamming of the door or overloading can shorten its lifespan.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The washing machine failed to fill with water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error code E02 indicates the Beko washing machine did not detect enough water entering the drum within the expected time frame (typically 8-10 minutes). The pressure switch or flow meter has not registered the required water level. This could be due to a closed water tap, kinked inlet hose, blocked inlet valve filter, faulty inlet valve solenoid, or a defective pressure switch or its associated tubing.

causes.cause: Water supply tap is closed or water pressure is too low
Inlet hose is kinked, crushed, or blocked
Inlet valve filter mesh is clogged with debris
Water inlet valve solenoid has failed
Pressure switch or pressure tube is faulty or disconnected

symptoms: Machine starts cycle but no water enters the drum
Very slow water fill with E02 eventually appearing
Humming or clicking sound from inlet valve area
E02 error displayed after several minutes of attempting to fill
Machine works intermittently depending on water pressure

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water tap is fully open. Check that other taps in the house have normal water pressure. If pressure is low throughout the house, wait for supply to normalize.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the tap and disconnect the inlet hose from both the tap and the machine. Check for kinks, blockages, or damage. Flush the hose with water to clear any debris.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet valve filter
fixSteps.instruction: At the back of the machine where the hose connects, carefully remove the small mesh filter screen using pliers. Clean it under running water, removing any sediment or limescale. Refit securely.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With power disconnected, access the inlet valve at the rear of the machine. Use a multimeter to test the solenoid coil resistance. It should read between 500-1500 ohms. An open circuit or very low reading indicates a failed valve.

fixSteps.title: Check pressure switch and tube
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the pressure switch (usually at the top of the machine). Check the small rubber tube connecting it to the drum for cracks, kinks, or blockages. Blow gently into the tube; you should hear a click from the switch.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve or pressure switch needs replacement and you are not comfortable disassembling the machine.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E02 error no water fill fix

faq.q: Why does my Beko washer show E02 intermittently?
faq.a: Intermittent E02 errors often point to low water pressure or a partially blocked inlet filter. The machine fills slowly and sometimes exceeds the timeout threshold.

faq.q: Can hard water cause the E02 error?
faq.a: Yes. Hard water causes limescale buildup in the inlet valve filter and the valve itself, gradually restricting water flow. Regular filter cleaning prevents this.

faq.q: How do I know if it is the inlet valve or the pressure switch?
faq.a: If no water enters the drum at all, the inlet valve is more likely. If water enters but the machine does not recognize the level, the pressure switch or its tube is suspect.

title: Drain Pump Failure
shortDescription: The washing machine cannot drain water from the drum within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error code E03 on Beko washers indicates a drainage fault. The control board detected that water was not pumped out of the drum within the expected time (usually 3-5 minutes). This triggers a safety stop to prevent overflow or motor damage from attempting to spin with a full drum. Common causes include a blocked drain pump filter, kinked drain hose, faulty pump motor, or a blockage in the sump or drain pipe.

causes.cause: Drain pump filter is clogged with debris, coins, or lint
Drain hose is kinked, blocked, or positioned too high
Drain pump impeller is jammed or broken
Drain pump motor has failed electrically
Sump hose between drum and pump is blocked

symptoms: Water remains in the drum at the end of a cycle
Machine stops mid-cycle with E03 displayed
Drain pump runs continuously but water does not drain
Burning smell from the pump area
Clothes are soaking wet after the final spin

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the drain pump filter access panel at the bottom front of the machine. Place towels and a shallow tray underneath. Open the panel, slowly unscrew the filter cap, and let water drain out. Remove any debris, coins, hairpins, or lint. Replace the filter and cap.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the machine away from the wall. Inspect the drain hose for kinks or crushing. The hose end should be between 60-90 cm above floor level. Disconnect the hose from the standpipe and check for blockages by flushing water through it.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, shine a torch into the pump housing and try to rotate the impeller by hand. It should spin freely in both directions. If it is jammed or blades are broken, the pump assembly needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump electrically
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the drain pump from the bottom or back of the machine. Disconnect the pump wiring and test resistance across the terminals with a multimeter. A good pump should read 100-300 ohms. Replace if open or shorted.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pump wiring connector and the hose clamps. Remove the mounting screws or twist-lock mechanism. Install the new pump, reconnect hoses and wiring, and test with a drain cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the pump motor needs replacing and you cannot access it from beneath the machine, or if the blockage is deep within the sump.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E03 error drain pump blocked fix

faq.q: Can I manually drain the water with an E03 error?
faq.a: Yes. Open the drain pump filter slowly with towels underneath to catch the water. You can also use the emergency drain hose if your model has one (small hose next to the filter).

faq.q: How often should I clean the Beko drain pump filter?
faq.a: Beko recommends cleaning the filter every 1-2 months, or more frequently if you wash items that shed a lot of lint or fibres.

faq.q: Why does E03 appear during the spin cycle specifically?
faq.a: The machine must drain before spinning. If drainage is slow or incomplete, the control board triggers E03 and cancels the spin to protect the motor and prevent vibration damage.

title: Overfill / Flood Protection Activated
shortDescription: The machine has detected too much water in the drum, triggering overfill protection.
fullDescription: Error code E04 on Beko washing machines indicates the water level has exceeded the maximum safe level. The overfill pressure switch or float switch has activated the flood protection system. The machine will stop filling and attempt to pump out excess water. This can be caused by a stuck-open inlet valve, a faulty pressure switch, or siphoning from an improperly installed drain hose.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve is stuck open and not closing when commanded
Pressure switch is faulty and not detecting the correct water level
Pressure switch tube is disconnected, cracked, or blocked
Drain hose siphon effect pulling water back into the drum

symptoms: Water visibly overflowing from the soap dispenser drawer
E04 error displayed and machine stops
Water leaking from beneath the machine
Drain pump runs continuously trying to remove excess water
Machine fills with water even when turned off

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Close the water tap supplying the machine. This is critical if the inlet valve is stuck open. If water continues to enter, the valve body may need replacing urgently.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose installation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the drain hose has a proper high loop or is connected to a standpipe at 60-90 cm height. If the hose drops below drum level, water can siphon back in. Use the hose hook provided with the machine.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pressure switch tube
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel. Locate the pressure switch and follow the small rubber tube down to the drum. Check for cracks, disconnections, or blockages. Reconnect or replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water supply. Remove the inlet valve from the rear of the machine. Test solenoid coil resistance with a multimeter (500-1500 ohms). Also check that the valve physically closes by blowing through it when unpowered; no air should pass.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pressure switch tube and blow into the switch port. You should hear a distinct click at a certain pressure. If no click, the switch is faulty and needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve is stuck open and water is continuously entering the machine, call a technician urgently to prevent flood damage.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E04 error overfill flood protection fix

faq.q: Is E04 an emergency?
faq.a: It can be. If water is continuously filling, turn off the water tap immediately. The machine's flood protection will try to pump out excess water, but you should intervene to prevent overflow.

faq.q: Why does my machine fill with water when it is turned off?
faq.a: This indicates a stuck-open inlet valve. The valve is not closing properly and water flows in by gravity from the mains. Turn off the tap when the machine is not in use and replace the valve.

faq.q: Can a siphon effect cause E04?
faq.a: Yes. If the drain hose end is lower than the water level in the drum, dirty water can siphon back in, causing the machine to think it is overfilling. Ensure the hose has a high loop.

title: Water Heating Failure
shortDescription: The washing machine's heating element has failed to heat the water to the target temperature.
fullDescription: Error code E05 on Beko washing machines indicates a failure in the water heating system. The NTC temperature sensor has not detected the expected temperature rise within the allotted time. This could be due to a burned-out heating element, a faulty NTC sensor, a wiring issue, or a control board fault. The machine may continue to wash with cold water or stop the cycle entirely depending on the model and firmware.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out or has an open circuit
NTC temperature sensor is giving incorrect readings
Limescale buildup on the heating element causing overheating and failure
Wiring connections to the heater or NTC are loose or corroded
Control board triac for the heater circuit has failed

symptoms: Clothes not washed effectively, especially on hot cycles
E05 error code on display panel
Cycle takes much longer than expected
Water feels cold when door is opened mid-cycle (on pause)
Machine completes cycle but laundry smells musty

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug from the mains for 5 minutes. Reconnect and run a 60 degree cycle. If E05 reappears, proceed with diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element (usually at the rear bottom of the drum). Disconnect the wiring spade connectors. Test resistance with a multimeter across the two main terminals. A good element should read 20-40 ohms. Also test between each terminal and the element body for earth leakage (should be open/infinite).

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The NTC sensor is usually embedded in or near the heating element. Disconnect it and measure resistance. At room temperature it should read approximately 20k-50k ohms (varies by model). Warm it gently; resistance should decrease smoothly.

fixSteps.title: Check for limescale
fixSteps.instruction: If the element is accessible, inspect it for heavy limescale coating. Thick limescale insulates the element, causing it to overheat and eventually fail. If not yet failed, descale with a commercial washing machine cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the retaining nut in the center of the element flange (do not remove fully, push inward to release the seal). Carefully pull the element out. Clean the seating area, insert the new element, and tighten the retaining nut. Reconnect wiring and NTC.

whenToCallPro: If the element tests fine but the error persists, indicating a possible control board fault, or if you are not comfortable removing the heating element.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E05 error heating element replacement

faq.q: Can I wash with cold water only if E05 appears?
faq.a: Some Beko models will allow cold wash cycles to complete. However, hot washes will fail. Detergent effectiveness drops significantly in cold water, so repair is recommended.

faq.q: How do I prevent heating element failure?
faq.a: Use a descaling product monthly in hard water areas. Run a hot maintenance wash (90 degrees) with no laundry once a month to prevent limescale and soap scum buildup.

faq.q: Is the heating element the same as in a kettle?
faq.a: Similar principle but different design. Washing machine elements are typically 1800-2200W tubular elements with an integrated NTC sensor, sealed to prevent water ingress through the drum.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor is providing out-of-range or erratic readings to the control board.
fullDescription: Error code E06 indicates the NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) thermistor used to measure water temperature is sending readings outside the expected parameters. The control board cannot accurately regulate water heating, so it halts the heating process as a safety measure. The sensor may be open circuit, short circuit, or drifting in value.

causes.cause: NTC sensor has failed (open or short circuit)
NTC sensor wiring is damaged or corroded
NTC sensor connector on the PCB is loose
Control board NTC input circuit has failed

symptoms: E06 error code displayed on control panel
Water overheating during wash cycles
Machine stops heating entirely
Inconsistent wash temperatures
Error appears intermittently and clears on reset

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug from the mains for 5 minutes. Reconnect and run a cycle. If E06 reappears, continue with diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Access and test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. The NTC sensor is usually located in or near the heating element at the bottom of the drum. Disconnect the sensor wires and test resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (20-25 C), expect 15k-50k ohms depending on model. An open circuit or zero ohms indicates failure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Trace the NTC sensor wires back to the control board connector. Look for corrosion, breaks, or loose pins. Clean connectors with electrical contact cleaner if corroded.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tested faulty, obtain a replacement. The NTC may be a separate component or integrated into the heating element housing. Remove the faulty sensor and install the replacement, ensuring a good seal.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring test fine but E06 persists, the control board input circuit may be damaged.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E06 NTC temperature sensor replacement

faq.q: Can a faulty NTC sensor cause the machine to overheat water?
faq.a: Yes. If the sensor reads lower than actual temperature, the control board will keep heating past the set temperature, potentially causing scalding hot wash water. This is why E06 should be repaired promptly.

faq.q: What does NTC stand for?
faq.a: NTC stands for Negative Temperature Coefficient. It means the sensor's electrical resistance decreases as temperature increases, which is how the control board measures water temperature.

faq.q: Is the NTC sensor expensive to replace?
faq.a: No, the sensor itself is typically under 20 pounds/euros. It is one of the cheaper parts to replace on a washing machine.

title: Motor Tacho / Hall Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The motor speed feedback sensor (tachometer) is not providing a signal to the control board.
fullDescription: Error code E07 on Beko washers indicates the control board is not receiving speed feedback from the motor tachometer (tacho) or Hall sensor. Without this feedback, the board cannot regulate drum speed during wash, rinse, and spin cycles. The machine will typically stop as it cannot safely control the motor. This fault is commonly caused by a disconnected tacho coil, a failed tacho generator, worn motor carbon brushes (on brushed motors), or a defective motor control section on the PCB.

causes.cause: Motor carbon brushes are worn below minimum length
Tachometer coil is disconnected or faulty
Tacho magnet on the motor rotor has come loose
Wiring between motor and control board is damaged
Motor inverter board has failed (brushless motors)

symptoms: E07 error code on display
Motor does not turn or turns very slowly
Drum spins at inconsistent speeds
Loud arcing or sparking sound from motor area
Machine hums but drum does not rotate

fixSteps.title: Reset the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug from the mains for 5 minutes and reconnect. If E07 persists, proceed with motor diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Check carbon brushes (brushed motors)
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the motor (usually from the rear panel). Remove each carbon brush holder. The brushes should be at least 15mm long. If shorter, they need replacing. Also check that the brushes slide freely in their holders.

fixSteps.title: Test the tachometer coil
fixSteps.instruction: With the motor accessible, locate the tacho coil at the rear of the motor. Disconnect it and measure resistance with a multimeter. Expected reading is 100-300 ohms. Also check for continuity in the tacho wiring back to the PCB.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the tacho magnet
fixSteps.instruction: The tacho magnet is attached to the motor rotor. Ensure it is firmly in place and not cracked. A loose magnet will give intermittent or no signal.

fixSteps.title: Replace carbon brushes or tacho
fixSteps.instruction: Install new carbon brushes by inserting them into the holders and securing the retaining clips. If the tacho coil is faulty, replace the entire tacho assembly. Run a test spin cycle.

whenToCallPro: If brushes and tacho are fine but E07 persists, the motor winding or control board inverter may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E07 error motor carbon brushes tacho fix

faq.q: How do I know if my Beko washer has a brushed or brushless motor?
faq.a: Most Beko models up to 2018 use brushed motors with carbon brushes. Newer models with inverter/ProSmart motors are brushless. Check for a sticker on the motor or your user manual.

faq.q: How long do carbon brushes last?
faq.a: Typically 5-10 years depending on usage. Heavy users may need replacement sooner. The brushes are a normal wear item.

faq.q: Can I replace carbon brushes myself?
faq.a: Yes, it is one of the more straightforward washing machine repairs. You need to access the motor from the rear and slide out the brush holders.

title: Motor Overcurrent / Overload
shortDescription: The motor is drawing excessive current, indicating an overload or short circuit condition.
fullDescription: Error code E08 indicates the washing machine motor is drawing more current than the control board allows. This triggers a protective shutdown to prevent motor damage, control board damage, or fire risk. Causes include an overloaded drum, a seized bearing, a jammed drum, a motor winding fault, or a failing control board triac.

causes.cause: Drum is overloaded with too much laundry
Drum bearings are worn or seized
Foreign object jammed between drum and outer tub
Motor winding has a partial short circuit
Control board motor triac is failing

symptoms: E08 error during wash or spin cycle
Machine struggles to turn the drum
Loud grinding or rumbling noise during spin
Burning smell from motor area
Circuit breaker trips when machine tries to spin

fixSteps.title: Reduce the load
fixSteps.instruction: If the drum is overloaded, remove some items and restart the cycle. Beko recommends not exceeding 75 percent of the drum capacity for cotton cycles and 50 percent for synthetics.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: With the machine empty and unplugged, try to rotate the drum by hand. It should turn freely with only slight resistance from the belt. If it grinds, catches, or is very stiff, there may be a foreign object or bearing failure.

fixSteps.title: Remove foreign objects
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the sump hose or heating element to access the gap between the inner drum and outer tub. Use a torch to look for underwire, coins, or other objects. Extract with long-nose pliers.

fixSteps.title: Assess drum bearings
fixSteps.instruction: Grab the drum through the door opening and try to lift it up and down. Significant play (more than 2-3mm) indicates worn bearings. Also spin the drum and listen for metallic grinding sounds.

whenToCallPro: If bearings are worn (this requires splitting the outer tub on most Beko models), or if the motor itself is suspected of being faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E08 error motor overload bearing fix

faq.q: Can overloading damage my Beko washer permanently?
faq.a: Repeated overloading accelerates bearing wear, stresses the motor, and can damage the suspension system. Always follow the recommended load capacity for each cycle type.

faq.q: How can I tell if the bearings are gone?
faq.a: Typical signs are a loud rumbling noise during spin, rust-colored water leaking from the rear, and excessive drum play when pushed up and down by hand.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing bearings or should I buy a new machine?
faq.a: Bearing replacement on Beko machines costs 150-300 professionally. If the machine is less than 5-6 years old, repair is usually worthwhile. Older machines may be better replaced.

title: Motor Direction / Triac Fault
shortDescription: The control board has detected a problem with the motor direction control circuit or triac.
fullDescription: Error code E09 indicates a fault in the motor direction-switching circuit on the control board. The washer drum needs to reverse direction periodically during the wash cycle. If the triac (electronic switch) controlling motor direction fails, or the motor winding has a fault preventing reversal, this error is triggered.

causes.cause: Motor control triac on the PCB has failed
Motor winding has a fault preventing direction change
Wiring between motor and control board is damaged
Carbon brushes worn unevenly causing poor contact in one direction

symptoms: E09 error on display
Drum only rotates in one direction
Motor buzzes but does not turn
Drum stops and starts repeatedly during wash
Error appears during wash phase but not spin

fixSteps.title: Reset the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 10 minutes. This allows the control board capacitors to discharge fully. Reconnect and test.

fixSteps.title: Check carbon brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Access the motor and inspect both carbon brushes. Even if one looks acceptable, if they are worn unevenly, the motor may not reverse properly. Replace both as a pair.

fixSteps.title: Test motor windings
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor wiring. Test resistance between each pair of motor terminals. Readings should be similar (within 10 percent). A large discrepancy suggests a winding fault.

fixSteps.title: Inspect control board
fixSteps.instruction: Visually inspect the PCB for burnt components, especially the triacs near the motor relay connections. Burnt or discolored components indicate failure. PCB repair or replacement is needed.

whenToCallPro: If the control board triac is suspected. PCB-level repair requires soldering skills and component knowledge.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E09 motor triac control board fix

faq.q: What is a triac?
faq.a: A triac is an electronic switch on the control board that controls power to the motor. It allows the board to switch motor direction and regulate speed without mechanical relays.

faq.q: Can the control board be repaired instead of replaced?
faq.a: Often yes. Specialist appliance PCB repair services can replace the failed triac component for less than the cost of a new board. Search for appliance PCB repair services in your area.

faq.q: Is E09 dangerous?
faq.a: Not immediately, but a failed triac can sometimes short circuit and overheat. If you notice a burning smell from the control board area, disconnect power immediately.

title: Motor Communication Error
shortDescription: The control board cannot communicate with the motor controller or inverter module.
fullDescription: Error code E10 indicates a communication failure between the main control board and the motor control module (on inverter/brushless motor models) or a general motor drive fault on brushed motor models. The main PCB sends commands but does not receive the expected response from the motor controller.

causes.cause: Wiring harness between main PCB and motor controller is loose
Motor inverter module has failed
Main control board communication port is faulty
Electromagnetic interference disrupting communication signals

symptoms: E10 error code on display
Motor does not respond at all
Machine powers on but cannot start any cycle
Error appears immediately when cycle is started
Intermittent E10 that clears after power reset

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. Check that the machine is not on a shared extension lead with high-power appliances that could cause interference. Plug directly into a wall socket.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the rear panel. Inspect all wiring connectors between the main PCB and the motor/motor controller. Reseat all connectors firmly, checking for corrosion or bent pins.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor controller board
fixSteps.instruction: If the machine has a separate motor inverter board (common on ProSmart inverter models), inspect it visually for burnt components, bulging capacitors, or scorch marks.

fixSteps.title: Test motor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the motor and test winding resistance. For brushless motors, test between each phase pair. Readings should be equal. Large differences indicate a motor winding fault.

whenToCallPro: If wiring checks are fine, a professional is needed to diagnose between the motor controller and main PCB.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E10 motor communication error fix

faq.q: What is an inverter motor?
faq.a: An inverter motor (Beko ProSmart) is a brushless motor that is quieter, more efficient, and longer-lasting than traditional brushed motors. It uses an electronic controller board to manage speed and direction.

faq.q: Can power surges cause E10?
faq.a: Yes. Power surges can damage the motor inverter board or disrupt communication. Using a surge protector is recommended for all modern washing machines.

faq.q: Is E10 repairable at home?
faq.a: If the issue is a loose wiring connector, yes. But if the inverter board or main PCB is faulty, professional diagnosis and repair is recommended.

title: Unbalanced Load Detection
shortDescription: The machine has detected an unbalanced load during the spin cycle and cannot reach full spin speed.
fullDescription: Error code E11 indicates the washing machine's load balancing system has detected a severely unbalanced load that it cannot correct. The machine attempts to redistribute the laundry by tumbling at low speed, but after multiple attempts, the imbalance persists. The machine will either reduce the spin speed significantly or cancel the spin entirely to prevent damage to the bearings, suspension, and cabinet.

causes.cause: Single heavy item (blanket, towel) bunched on one side
Too few items in the drum for the selected spin speed
Shock absorbers or dampers are worn
Suspension springs are broken or detached
Concrete counterweight is loose or cracked

symptoms: E11 error displayed during spin cycle
Machine vibrates excessively during spin
Clothes come out very wet as spin was reduced or cancelled
Banging or thumping sounds during spin attempt
Machine walks or moves across the floor

fixSteps.title: Redistribute the load
fixSteps.instruction: Pause the cycle, open the door (drain may be needed first), and rearrange the laundry evenly around the drum. Mix heavy and light items. Avoid washing a single large item alone.

fixSteps.title: Check machine levelling
fixSteps.instruction: Place a spirit level on top of the machine. Adjust the four feet until the machine is perfectly level. Tighten the lock nuts against the base. An unlevel machine is more prone to imbalance errors.

fixSteps.title: Inspect shock absorbers
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear or bottom panel. Push down firmly on the drum and release. It should return slowly to position without bouncing. If it bounces, the shock absorbers are worn. Check each absorber for oil leakage or loss of resistance.

fixSteps.title: Check suspension springs
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the springs that support the drum from the top. Ensure all are attached and none are stretched, broken, or missing. Replace any damaged springs.

whenToCallPro: If E11 persists despite load redistribution and machine levelling, the suspension components likely need professional assessment.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E11 unbalanced load vibration fix

faq.q: Why does E11 happen with duvets and blankets?
faq.a: Large bulky items tend to bunch up on one side of the drum. Wash them with a couple of towels to help balance, and use the dedicated bedding/duvet cycle if available.

faq.q: Can removing the transit bolts fix E11?
faq.a: If the transit bolts were never removed after installation, the drum cannot move freely and will generate constant imbalance errors. Check and remove all transit bolts from the rear.

faq.q: Is E11 harmful to the machine?
faq.a: Occasional E11 is normal. However, frequent imbalance indicates worn suspension components that should be replaced to prevent damage to bearings and the cabinet.

title: Control Board EEPROM Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected a memory (EEPROM) read or write error.
fullDescription: Error code E12 indicates the main control board's EEPROM (non-volatile memory chip) has a fault. The EEPROM stores cycle parameters, calibration data, and error history. If this data becomes corrupted or the chip fails, the machine cannot operate correctly. Power surges, electrical noise, or component aging can cause this fault.

causes.cause: EEPROM chip on the control board is corrupted
Power surge has damaged the memory circuit
Control board solder joints have cracked (dry joints)
Moisture ingress onto the control board

symptoms: E12 error on display, machine will not start
Machine loses programmed settings after power off
Random cycle selection or incorrect cycle behaviour
Display shows garbled or incorrect information
Error appears immediately at power on

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 15 minutes. This allows full capacitor discharge and may clear a temporary EEPROM glitch. Reconnect and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the control board cover. Inspect the PCB for any signs of moisture, condensation, or water marks. If wet, dry thoroughly with compressed air and allow to air dry for 24 hours before retesting.

fixSteps.title: Visual PCB inspection
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the control board under bright light for cracked solder joints (especially around large connectors), burnt components, or corroded tracks. Pay attention to the EEPROM chip area (small 8-pin IC).

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the EEPROM is corrupt and cannot be reprogrammed, the control board needs replacement. Note the exact board part number from the label on the PCB. Transfer any jumper settings from the old board to the new one.

whenToCallPro: EEPROM faults almost always require professional diagnosis and board replacement or reprogramming.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E12 EEPROM control board error fix

faq.q: Can the EEPROM be reprogrammed?
faq.a: Some specialist repair services can reprogram the EEPROM with the correct firmware. This is cheaper than a new board but requires specialist equipment and the correct software file.

faq.q: Will a power surge protector prevent E12?
faq.a: A good surge protector can help prevent EEPROM corruption from power surges. It is recommended for all modern washing machines with electronic control boards.

faq.q: Is E12 repairable at home?
faq.a: A power reset may clear a temporary glitch. But a genuine EEPROM failure requires professional repair or board replacement.

title: Mains Voltage Out of Range
shortDescription: The control board has detected that the incoming mains voltage is too high or too low.
fullDescription: Error code E13 indicates the Beko washing machine's power supply monitoring circuit has detected an abnormal mains voltage. European models expect 220-240V AC. If the voltage drops below approximately 190V or rises above 260V, the machine will trigger this error to protect its electronic components and motor.

causes.cause: Household mains voltage is unstable or out of specification
Machine is connected to an overloaded circuit or extension lead
Faulty mains power cable or plug
Control board voltage sensing circuit is faulty

symptoms: E13 displayed on control panel
Machine will not start or shuts down mid-cycle
Error appears during high-power operations (heating, spinning)
Lights in the house flicker when the machine operates
Error is intermittent and depends on time of day

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Plug the machine directly into a wall socket, not an extension lead. Ensure no other high-power appliances (electric heaters, kettles) are on the same circuit.

fixSteps.title: Test mains voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter set to AC voltage, carefully measure the voltage at the wall socket. It should read between 220-240V AC. Readings outside 200-250V indicate a mains supply issue.

fixSteps.title: Check the power cable
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the machine's mains cable for damage, kinks, or overheating at the plug. Test continuity through each core of the cable. A damaged cable can cause voltage drop.

fixSteps.title: Contact an electrician
fixSteps.instruction: If the mains voltage is genuinely out of range, contact a qualified electrician to investigate. The issue may be with your household wiring, consumer unit, or the local electricity supply.

whenToCallPro: If mains voltage readings are abnormal, call a qualified electrician. Do not attempt to repair household wiring yourself.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E13 voltage error mains supply fix

faq.q: Can a voltage stabilizer fix E13?
faq.a: Yes, if your local mains voltage is consistently unstable, an automatic voltage stabilizer (AVR) rated for at least 3000W can protect the machine and prevent E13 errors.

faq.q: Why does E13 appear only at certain times?
faq.a: Mains voltage often drops during peak usage times (evenings) when many households are drawing power. If your area has marginal supply, the voltage may dip below the machine's threshold during peak hours.

faq.q: Is running the machine on low voltage dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. Low voltage causes the motor to draw excess current, leading to overheating. It can also damage the control board electronics. The E13 error is a protective measure.

title: Drum Detection / Speed Sensor Error
shortDescription: The control board cannot detect the drum position or speed during the initial calibration phase.
fullDescription: Error code E17 appears when the control board's drum detection routine fails. At the start of certain cycles, the machine performs a brief calibration to determine load weight and drum position. If the speed sensor or motor feedback does not respond correctly during this phase, E17 is triggered.

causes.cause: Drive belt is broken or slipped off the pulley
Motor tacho sensor is disconnected
Motor carbon brushes too short to make good contact
Drum is physically jammed and cannot rotate

symptoms: E17 error appears at the start of a cycle
Drum does not move during wash
Motor hums but drum is stationary
Burning rubber smell from broken belt
Error appears after the machine has been idle for a period

fixSteps.title: Check the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel. Check if the drive belt is still on the motor and drum pulleys. If it has snapped or slipped off, it will be visible. A worn belt may be glazed and slipping without breaking.

fixSteps.title: Replace or refit the belt
fixSteps.instruction: If the belt is broken, fit a new one. Loop it over the motor pulley first, then walk it onto the drum pulley while turning the drum by hand. The belt should be tight with minimal deflection.

fixSteps.title: Check motor tacho and brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the motor tachometer connection. Check carbon brush length (minimum 15mm). Replace brushes if worn.

fixSteps.title: Check for drum obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Try to rotate the drum by hand through the door opening. It should turn freely. If jammed, a foreign object may be caught between the drums.

whenToCallPro: If the belt and motor components are fine but E17 persists, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E17 drive belt drum detection error fix

faq.q: How often do Beko drive belts break?
faq.a: Drive belts typically last 5-10 years. Overloading and high spin speeds accelerate wear. A squealing noise during spin often precedes belt failure.

faq.q: Can I use a generic belt?
faq.a: It is best to use the correct size belt. Check the belt size printed on your old belt (e.g., 1245 J5) and match it exactly. A wrong size belt will slip or be too tight.

faq.q: Why does E17 appear after the machine has been sitting unused?
faq.a: If unused for months, the belt can develop flat spots that prevent smooth rotation during calibration. Running a cycle usually works the flat spot out.

title: Excessive Foam Detection
shortDescription: The machine has detected excessive foam (suds) in the drum, which interferes with proper operation.
fullDescription: Error code E18 indicates the foam detection system has triggered due to excessive suds in the drum. Too much detergent, wrong detergent type (non-HE detergent in an HE machine), or very soft water can cause excessive foaming. The machine will attempt extra rinse cycles and drain cycles to reduce foam before resuming normal operation.

causes.cause: Too much detergent used for the load size
Non-HE detergent used in a high-efficiency machine
Very soft water area causing excess lather
Detergent dispenser blocked causing sudden release
Pressure switch misreading foam as water level

symptoms: E18 error displayed on control panel
Visible foam leaking from the soap drawer or door seal
Machine runs extra rinse cycles automatically
Cycle takes much longer than expected
Drain pump runs repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Wait for automatic recovery
fixSteps.instruction: The machine will typically attempt to resolve the foam issue by running extra rinse and drain cycles. Wait for 15-20 minutes and let the machine try to clear the foam automatically.

fixSteps.title: Run a rinse and spin cycle
fixSteps.instruction: If the machine has cleared the error, run a rinse and spin cycle with no detergent to flush remaining suds from the system.

fixSteps.title: Clean the detergent dispenser
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the detergent drawer and wash it thoroughly under running water. Clean the dispenser housing cavity with a brush to remove any buildup that might be causing detergent to release unevenly.

fixSteps.title: Reduce detergent dose
fixSteps.instruction: For future washes, use the correct amount of HE-rated detergent. In soft water areas, reduce the dose by 30-50 percent from the package recommendation. Use liquid detergent tablets if unsure of dosing.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed for E18. If the error persists with correct detergent dosing, the pressure switch may be misreading foam as water level.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E18 too much foam suds error fix

faq.q: How much detergent should I use in my Beko washer?
faq.a: Follow the detergent manufacturer's dosing guide for your water hardness and load size. For most Beko machines, use the low-dose marking on the detergent cap. Less is often more.

faq.q: What is HE detergent?
faq.a: HE (High Efficiency) detergent is formulated to produce less foam. It is essential for modern front-loading machines. Look for the HE symbol on the packaging.

faq.q: Can fabric softener cause E18?
faq.a: Excessive fabric softener can contribute to foaming, especially if it leaks into the wash cycle rather than the rinse. Use the recommended amount and clean the dispenser regularly.

title: Water Level Sensor Timeout
shortDescription: The water level sensor has not detected the expected change in water level during filling or draining.
fullDescription: Error code E20 indicates a timeout in the water level monitoring system. The pressure switch has not registered the expected water level change within the allotted time during a fill or drain operation. This may overlap with E02 or E03 causes but specifically relates to the pressure sensing system.

causes.cause: Pressure switch tube is kinked, blocked, or disconnected
Pressure switch membrane is damaged or stuck
Air trap at the drum connection point is blocked with detergent residue
Pressure switch wiring is faulty

symptoms: E20 error on display
Machine fills but does not start washing
Machine overfills or underfills
Cycle stops at an unexpected point
Multiple error codes appearing in sequence

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pressure tube
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel. Follow the thin rubber tube from the pressure switch down to the drum. Check for kinks, cracks, or disconnections at either end. Reconnect or replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Clear the air trap
fixSteps.instruction: Locate where the pressure tube connects to the outer drum (air trap/dome). Remove the tube and clean the air trap nipple with a thin wire or pipe cleaner to remove any detergent residue blocking the air passage.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the pressure tube from the switch. Attach a short piece of tubing and blow gently. You should hear a distinct click when air pressure reaches the threshold. Release and hear another click. If no clicks, the switch is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the mounting screw or clip. Disconnect the electrical connector and tube. Fit the new switch, reconnect the tube and electrical connector.

whenToCallPro: If the pressure switch and tube are in good condition but E20 persists, the control board may have an issue.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer E20 water level pressure switch error fix

faq.q: What is the air trap and why does it block?
faq.a: The air trap is a small chamber where the pressure tube connects to the drum. Detergent residue and limescale can accumulate, blocking the air passage. Regular hot maintenance washes help prevent this.

faq.q: Can I use any rubber tube as a replacement?
faq.a: Use the correct diameter tube. Too large and it will not seal; too small and it will restrict airflow. The original Beko tube is recommended.

faq.q: Is the pressure switch the same as the water level switch?
faq.a: Yes, they are the same component. It uses air pressure from the drum to determine the water level.

title: Drying Sensor Fault (Washer-Dryer)
shortDescription: The drying temperature or humidity sensor in the washer-dryer combo is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error code E21 appears on Beko washer-dryer combination units when the drying system sensor fails. The sensor monitors the outlet air temperature or humidity to determine drying progress. If it sends out-of-range signals, the machine cannot accurately control the drying cycle and triggers this error.

causes.cause: Drying temperature sensor has failed
Condensation duct is blocked with lint
Sensor wiring is damaged or disconnected
Drying heater element has failed

symptoms: E21 error during drying cycle
Clothes remain damp after drying cycle
Drying cycle runs indefinitely without stopping
Machine overheats during drying
Error does not appear during wash-only cycles

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser and ducts
fixSteps.instruction: Access the condenser at the bottom of the machine. Remove and clean it under running water. Also clean the lint filter in the door seal area. Blocked airways cause inaccurate sensor readings.

fixSteps.title: Check the drying sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the drying NTC sensor (usually in the drying duct or near the condenser). Test resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature, expect 10k-50k ohms. Out-of-range readings indicate failure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drying heater
fixSteps.instruction: Test the drying heater element for continuity and correct resistance. A failed heater will prevent proper drying and may cause sensor errors due to no temperature rise.

fixSteps.title: Replace the faulty sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the drying sensor tested out of range, replace it with the correct part. Reconnect wiring and run a test drying cycle.

whenToCallPro: If sensor and heater test fine, the drying control circuitry on the main PCB may be at fault.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer dryer E21 drying sensor error fix

faq.q: Does E21 only affect the drying function?
faq.a: Yes. The washing function should continue to work normally. You can use wash-only cycles while awaiting repair of the drying sensor.

faq.q: How often should I clean the condenser on a washer-dryer?
faq.a: Every 1-2 months, or after every 20-30 drying cycles. A clogged condenser reduces drying efficiency and stresses the sensors.

faq.q: My machine is only a washer, why am I seeing E21?
faq.a: E21 should not appear on wash-only models. If it does, it may be a control board glitch. Try a power reset.

title: Main PCB Hardware Fault
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal hardware fault.
fullDescription: Error code H01 indicates the main control board (PCB) has detected an internal hardware failure during its self-diagnostic routine at startup. This could be a failed microprocessor, corrupted firmware, damaged power supply regulator, or other critical component on the board.

causes.cause: Power supply regulator on the PCB has failed
Microprocessor malfunction due to power surge
Dry solder joints causing intermittent connections
Lightning strike or severe voltage spike damage

symptoms: H01 error displayed immediately at power on
Machine is completely unresponsive
Display flickers or shows random characters then H01
No buttons or controls respond
Intermittent startup followed by H01

fixSteps.title: Complete power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the machine for 30 minutes for a thorough capacitor discharge. This can sometimes resolve a temporary microprocessor lockup. Reconnect and check.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Verify mains voltage at the wall socket is within specification (220-240V AC). An abnormal supply can cause board-level issues.

fixSteps.title: Visual board inspection
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the control board cover. Inspect the PCB for visibly damaged components: bulging capacitors, burnt resistors, discolored areas, or cracked solder joints.

fixSteps.title: Replace the main PCB
fixSteps.instruction: If the board has visible damage or H01 persists, the main PCB requires replacement. Note the exact board part number and any configuration jumpers before ordering.

whenToCallPro: H01 almost always requires professional board replacement or repair. PCB repair services may be able to fix specific components for less than a new board.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer H01 control board PCB replacement

faq.q: Can a PCB repair service fix H01 cheaper than a new board?
faq.a: Often yes. Specialist appliance PCB repair services charge 40-80 pounds and can replace specific failed components. This is usually cheaper than a new board at 100-200 pounds.

faq.q: Is H01 dangerous?
faq.a: Not directly, but a failing PCB could potentially cause incorrect operation of other components. It is best not to use the machine until repaired.

faq.q: How can I prevent PCB failures?
faq.a: Use a surge protector, ensure proper ventilation around the control board area, and avoid operating the machine during thunderstorms.

title: Display Board Communication Error
shortDescription: The main control board and display/user interface board cannot communicate properly.
fullDescription: Error code H02 indicates a communication breakdown between the main control board and the front display panel board. These two boards communicate via a serial data link. If the cable is loose, damaged, or either board has a communication port fault, the machine cannot accept user inputs or display status information correctly.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable or wiring between boards is loose or disconnected
Display board has failed
Main PCB communication port is faulty
Connector pins are corroded or bent

symptoms: H02 error on display (if partially working)
Display is blank but machine powers on
Buttons do not respond to touch
Display shows random segments or garbled text
Machine starts but cannot be controlled

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes and reconnect. A temporary communication glitch may clear.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the communication cable
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the top panel or front control panel fascia to access the wiring between boards. Disconnect and firmly reconnect the ribbon cable or multi-pin connector at both the display board and main PCB.

fixSteps.title: Inspect connector pins
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the connector pins on both boards for corrosion, bent pins, or damage. Clean with electrical contact cleaner if corroded.

fixSteps.title: Replace the display board
fixSteps.instruction: If the cable and connectors are fine, the display board may need replacing. Unscrew the display module, disconnect wiring, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables does not resolve H02, professional diagnosis is needed to determine which board is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer H02 display board communication error fix

faq.q: Can I use the machine if the display is blank but it powers on?
faq.a: Without a functioning display, you cannot safely select cycles or monitor the machine. It is not recommended to use the machine until the display is repaired.

faq.q: Are display boards interchangeable between Beko models?
faq.a: No. Display boards are model-specific due to different features and firmware. Always match the exact part number from your machine.

faq.q: Could condensation cause H02?
faq.a: Yes, especially in humid environments. If the machine is in a damp location, condensation can form on connectors and cause communication errors.

title: Relay Circuit Fault on PCB
shortDescription: A relay on the main control board has failed or is stuck.
fullDescription: Error code H03 indicates one of the power relays on the main PCB has failed. Relays control high-power components like the heater, motor, and inlet valve. A stuck relay can cause components to operate continuously or not at all, creating safety risks.

causes.cause: Relay contacts are welded closed (stuck on)
Relay coil has burned out (stuck off)
Component controlled by the relay has a short circuit
PCB trace damage around the relay

symptoms: H03 error displayed on control panel
Heater runs continuously or not at all
Motor does not respond to commands
Inlet valve stays open or will not open
Burning smell from the control board area

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If a relay is stuck on, a component may be running continuously. Unplug the machine immediately and turn off the water supply if the inlet valve may be stuck open.

fixSteps.title: Identify the affected relay
fixSteps.instruction: Note which component was misbehaving before the error. This helps identify which relay has failed. Common relays control: heater, motor, inlet valve, drain pump.

fixSteps.title: Visual board inspection
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the PCB cover and inspect the relay area for scorch marks, discoloration, or melted plastic on relay housings. Failed relays often show visible damage.

fixSteps.title: Professional PCB repair or replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Relay replacement on a PCB requires desoldering and soldering skills. A PCB repair service can replace the specific relay, or the entire board can be replaced.

whenToCallPro: H03 requires professional repair in virtually all cases. Do not attempt to operate the machine with a stuck relay.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer H03 relay fault control board repair

faq.q: Is a stuck relay dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. A relay stuck in the on position can cause a component to run continuously, potentially leading to overheating, flooding, or fire. Disconnect power immediately when H03 appears.

faq.q: How much does a relay repair cost?
faq.a: A specialist PCB repair typically costs 40-80 pounds for relay replacement. A complete new board costs 100-200 pounds. Both options are viable depending on availability.

faq.q: What causes relays to fail?
faq.a: Relays fail from normal wear (they have a finite number of switching cycles), power surges, or excessive current from a short-circuiting component they control.

title: Heater Relay Stuck On
shortDescription: The heater relay is stuck in the ON position, causing continuous heating.
fullDescription: Error code H05 specifically indicates the relay controlling the heating element is stuck closed. The control board has detected that the water temperature continues to rise even after commanding the heater to turn off. This is a safety-critical error as continuous heating can cause dangerously hot water and potential scalding.

causes.cause: Heater relay contacts have welded together
Triac controlling the heater has failed short-circuit
PCB track damage creating a short across the relay
NTC sensor giving false readings making it appear heater is stuck on

symptoms: H05 error on display
Water becomes extremely hot during wash
Steam visible from the machine
Machine shuts down as a safety precaution
Burning or melting smell

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power immediately
fixSteps.instruction: This is a safety-critical error. Unplug the machine from the mains immediately. Do not open the door as water may be scalding hot.

fixSteps.title: Allow to cool
fixSteps.instruction: Wait at least 30 minutes for the water to cool before opening the door. If the machine has a drain function accessible, drain the hot water first.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Before assuming a relay fault, test the NTC sensor. A faulty sensor giving low readings could make the board think it needs to keep heating. Test resistance at room temperature.

fixSteps.title: Professional PCB repair
fixSteps.instruction: If the NTC tests fine, the heater relay or triac on the PCB has failed. This requires professional repair. Do not use the machine until repaired.

whenToCallPro: Immediately. H05 is a safety-critical error. Do not attempt to use the machine until it has been professionally repaired.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer H05 heater relay stuck on repair

faq.q: Is H05 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. Continuous heating can cause water to reach near-boiling temperatures, damaging clothes, seals, and potentially causing burns. Disconnect power immediately.

faq.q: Can I just disconnect the heater and keep using the machine?
faq.a: While technically possible for cold washes, this bypasses the safety system. It is strongly recommended to have the PCB repaired properly.

faq.q: How does the machine detect a stuck-on heater?
faq.a: The NTC sensor monitors water temperature. If temperature continues rising after the heater command is turned off, the control board knows the relay is stuck and triggers H05.

title: Motor Speed Sensor Calibration Error
shortDescription: The motor speed sensor failed its calibration check during machine startup.
fullDescription: Error code H07 indicates that during the startup self-test, the motor speed feedback system could not be calibrated. The control board sends a test pulse to the motor and expects a specific response from the tacho sensor. If the response is absent or incorrect, H07 is triggered.

causes.cause: Tachometer sensor is failing or intermittent
Motor brushes too worn for reliable low-speed operation
Belt is slipping during calibration pulse
Motor inverter board calibration data is corrupted

symptoms: H07 error appears at the start of a cycle
Machine will not proceed past the initial startup phase
Motor makes a brief attempt to turn then stops
Error is intermittent and sometimes clears on retry
Machine works fine once it gets past the startup check

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the machine
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and try again. If H07 appears intermittently, it may clear on retry.

fixSteps.title: Check the drive belt tension
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the rear panel. Check that the belt is properly seated on both pulleys and has adequate tension. A loose or worn belt will slip during the calibration pulse.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor brushes and tacho
fixSteps.instruction: Check carbon brush length and tacho sensor connections. Even slightly worn brushes can cause poor contact during the low-speed calibration test.

fixSteps.title: Replace worn components
fixSteps.instruction: Replace brushes if under 15mm, replace belt if worn or stretched, and replace tacho if it tests out of specification.

whenToCallPro: If all mechanical components are fine but H07 persists, the motor inverter board may need recalibration or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer H07 motor calibration speed sensor error fix

faq.q: Why does H07 only appear sometimes?
faq.a: The calibration test requires precise motor feedback. Marginal components (slightly worn brushes or a weakening tacho) may pass sometimes and fail at other times depending on temperature and brush contact position.

faq.q: Can I skip the calibration test?
faq.a: No. The calibration is built into the firmware and cannot be bypassed by the user. It is a safety check to ensure proper motor control.

faq.q: Is H07 the same as E07?
faq.a: They are related but different. E07 occurs during operation when speed feedback is lost. H07 occurs during the startup calibration check before a cycle begins.

title: Aqua Protect / Leak Detection
shortDescription: The machine's leak detection system (AquaProtect) has been triggered.
fullDescription: Error code H11 indicates the AquaProtect flood protection system has detected water in the base tray of the machine. A float switch in the base tray rises when water accumulates, triggering the drain pump and shutting down all water intake. This is a critical safety feature to prevent household flooding.

causes.cause: Internal hose has a leak or loose clamp
Door seal (gasket) is torn or not seated properly
Drum seal or bearing seal is leaking
Soap dispenser housing is cracked
Float switch is stuck in the raised position

symptoms: H11 error displayed on control panel
Drain pump runs continuously
Water visible underneath the machine
Machine will not fill with water
Error reappears immediately after reset

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Close the water tap and unplug the machine. Mop up any water around the machine.

fixSteps.title: Tilt and check the base tray
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the machine back to access the base. Check for water in the base tray. If present, identify the source of the leak by looking for drip trails. Soak up the water with towels.

fixSteps.title: Check internal hoses and clamps
fixSteps.instruction: With the machine tilted, inspect all internal hoses (sump to pump, pump to drain, inlet to dispenser). Look for loose hose clamps, cracked hoses, or drip marks. Tighten or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check the door seal (gasket) for tears, cracks, or foreign objects causing a poor seal. Run your hand around the entire seal looking for damage. Also check the seal is properly seated in the lip.

fixSteps.title: Reset the float switch
fixSteps.instruction: If water has been removed but the float switch is stuck up, gently press it down. Test by running a short cycle and monitoring for leaks.

whenToCallPro: If the leak is from the drum bearings area (rear of drum), professional repair is needed as bearing replacement requires splitting the tub.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer H11 AquaProtect leak detection error fix

faq.q: Can I reset H11 and keep using the machine?
faq.a: You can reset it by draining the base tray and pressing the float switch down, but this is only temporary. The leak must be found and fixed or the error will return and flooding may occur.

faq.q: Is H11 always a real leak?
faq.a: Usually yes, but a stuck float switch or condensation in humid environments can occasionally trigger a false H11. Always check for actual water first.

faq.q: Does every Beko washer have AquaProtect?
faq.a: Most Beko models from 2015 onwards include the AquaProtect system. Older models may not have the float switch and base tray protection.

title: Door Lock Feedback Error
shortDescription: The door lock feedback signal does not match the expected state.
fullDescription: Error code H12 is a more specific door lock fault than E01. It indicates the door lock's feedback circuit reports a different state than what the control board commanded. For example, the board commanded the lock to engage but the feedback says it is still open, or vice versa. This discrepancy triggers H12.

causes.cause: Door lock feedback microswitch has failed
Door lock actuator is stuck in one position
Feedback wiring is damaged
Main PCB lock control circuit fault

symptoms: H12 error on display
Door appears to lock but machine does not start
Door does not unlock after cycle completion
Machine starts without the door being fully locked
Error appears intermittently

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes. The door lock has a thermal element that takes several minutes to cool and release. After reconnecting, check if the lock operates correctly.

fixSteps.title: Check door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the door latch is engaging properly with the lock mechanism. Adjust the door hinge or strike if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and access the door lock. Test the feedback circuit with a multimeter. The feedback switch should change state when the lock mechanism is activated.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: If the feedback mechanism is faulty, replace the entire door lock assembly. Peel back the door seal, unscrew the lock, disconnect wiring, and fit the new unit.

whenToCallPro: If the lock replacement does not resolve H12, the control board's lock control circuit may be at fault.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer H12 door lock feedback error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E01 and H12?
faq.a: E01 is a general door lock failure (lock does not engage). H12 is a feedback mismatch (lock may engage but the feedback signal does not match). H12 suggests a more specific fault in the lock's internal switch.

faq.q: Can a door lock be stuck closed?
faq.a: Yes. The thermal element in the lock can fail in the locked position. Wait 10 minutes after unplugging for it to cool and release. If it remains stuck, the lock must be forced or replaced.

faq.q: Is H12 a safety concern?
faq.a: Yes. If the feedback incorrectly reports the door as locked when it is not, the machine could start with the door open. The H12 error prevents this scenario.

title: No Water Supply Detected
shortDescription: The machine is not receiving any water supply at all.
fullDescription: Error code H20 (which visually resembles H2O, the chemical formula for water) indicates a complete absence of water supply to the machine. Unlike E02 which is a timeout, H20 means the flow sensor or pressure switch detected absolutely no water entry from the start. This is typically a simple supply issue rather than an internal fault.

causes.cause: Water supply tap is completely closed
Water supply has been interrupted (local outage)
Inlet hose is completely blocked or frozen
Inlet valve has failed completely

symptoms: H20 displayed on control panel
Machine makes no water filling sounds
No water in the drum
Cycle will not start
Error appears quickly after starting a cycle

fixSteps.title: Check the water tap
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water supply tap is fully open. Some self-closing taps may have shut themselves. Turn the tap off and on again.

fixSteps.title: Check household water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Open another tap in the house to verify water supply is available. If no water is flowing anywhere, there may be a local supply outage or a frozen pipe.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the inlet hose and check for blockages. In cold weather, the hose may have frozen if exposed to sub-zero temperatures. Thaw carefully if frozen.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If water supply is confirmed but no water enters, the inlet valve may have failed. Test the solenoid with a multimeter for continuity.

whenToCallPro: H20 is usually resolved by opening the water tap. If the inlet valve needs replacing and you are not comfortable, call a technician.
videoSearchQuery: Beko washer H20 no water supply error fix

faq.q: Why is the error code H20 instead of a number?
faq.a: H20 is a mnemonic that resembles H2O (water). Beko uses this distinctive code to indicate the most basic water supply issue, making it easy for users to identify.

faq.q: Can a power outage cause H20?
faq.a: A power outage itself would not cause H20, but if a motorized water valve (like a whole-house shutoff) closed during the outage and did not reopen, you would get no water supply.

faq.q: My tap is open but I still get H20. What now?
faq.a: Check the inlet hose filter for blockage, ensure the hose is not kinked, and verify water pressure is adequate. If all external factors are fine, the inlet valve solenoid has likely failed.
